title: ErrorEnvelope
description: Canonical error response shape shared by every public service surface (HTTP and WebSocket). Source plan: docs/implementation-plans/29-rate-limiting-and-secret-management-plan.md (Critical Fix 4). Doctrine: docs/operations/error-envelope.md. The `scope` field is intentionally coarse to deny attackers an oracle on the exact bucket key; per-bucket fields (`bucketKey`, `remaining`, `limit`, `currentCount`) are forbidden by `additionalProperties: false`. See docs/architecture/error-schema-map.md for the layer / consumer / owning-plan map across all error-shaped schemas.

error.description: Closed error code. Maps 1:1 to HTTP status (see doctrine § HTTP mapping) and to the signaling `ERROR { code }` discriminator.

retryAfterMs.description: Hint to the client. Present only when retry is meaningful (`RATE_LIMITED`, `BUDGET_EXCEEDED` after window reset, `SERVICE_PAUSED`, `UNAVAILABLE`). Mirrors HTTP `Retry-After` (in milliseconds rather than seconds for finer client-side scheduling). Coarse-grained: never reveals the exact remaining-tokens count.

scope.description: Coarse axis that triggered the failure. Closed enum forbids leaking the exact bucket key so an attacker cannot calibrate the rate-limit shape against this response. Per-bucket detail (e.g., the actual key, the remaining-tokens count) is intentionally absent.

requestId.description: UUID v4 generated server-side. Carried in `requestId` header on HTTP responses and in the signaling `ERROR` payload. Used by support to correlate a user report with server logs without exposing user-identifying data.
